SECRETS.name: All Plugins
SECRETS.description: Enable every detect-secrets plugin — maximum recall across cloud providers, SaaS tokens, entropy strings, private keys, and keyword heuristics. Good starting point; tune confidence_threshold or disable noisy plugins for production.

SECRETS.name: Cloud Provider Keys
SECRETS.description: High-precision detection of structured cloud credentials: AWS keys, GitHub/GitLab tokens, Slack bot tokens, OpenAI keys, Stripe live keys, Azure storage keys, PyPI tokens, and PEM private keys. Near-zero false positives.

SECRETS.name: SaaS & Developer Tokens
SECRETS.description: Broad SaaS credential coverage: Artifactory, Discord, IBM Cloud, JWT, Mailchimp, npm, SendGrid, Square, Telegram, and Twilio tokens. Complements Cloud Provider Keys for full third-party secret coverage.

SECRETS.name: Entropy Scanner
SECRETS.description: Detect high-entropy strings that don't match known token patterns — catches custom secrets and randomly generated passwords. Higher false-positive rate; best paired with structured plugin scans.

SECRETS.name: Entropy Scanner — Strict
SECRETS.description: Tightened entropy thresholds to reduce false positives at the cost of recall. Suitable for CI/CD enforcement pipelines where manual review is unavailable.

SECRETS.name: Keyword Heuristics
SECRETS.description: Flag variable assignments whose names suggest secrets (password=, api_key=, secret=). High false-positive rate by design — treat as candidates for human review, not confirmed findings.

SECRETS.name: IP Address Exposure
SECRETS.description: Detect public IP addresses in configs, code, or documents. Useful for data governance audits where internal network topology must not leak externally.

SECRETS.name: Enterprise Full Sweep
SECRETS.description: All structured credential plugins plus entropy scanning at balanced thresholds. Designed for scheduled scans across large document corpora where review capacity exists.

PII.name: Global Coverage
PII.description: All globally-recognized PII patterns: emails, phones, credit cards, crypto wallets, IPs, IBANs, URLs, and named entities (persons, locations, dates). Good baseline for any data source.

PII.name: US Compliance (SOC 2 / HIPAA)
PII.description: US government and financial identifiers: SSN, driver licenses, passports, ITIN, and bank accounts. Pair with Global Coverage for complete US compliance.

PII.name: GDPR (European Union)
PII.description: GDPR-relevant entity types: EU national identifiers, IBAN codes, medical licenses, and contact details. Designed for Article 5 data minimization and DPIA workflows.

PII.name: DACH (Germany, Austria, Switzerland)
PII.description: DACH-specific identifiers: German tax IDs, Austrian social insurance, Swiss AHV numbers, plus EU-wide patterns for DSGVO/DSG compliance.

PII.name: Spain
PII.description: Spanish national identifiers: NIF for citizens and NIE for foreign residents, combined with contact patterns.

PII.name: Italy
PII.description: Italian identifiers: fiscal codes, passports, driver licenses, identity cards, and VAT codes for Codice della Privacy compliance.

PII.name: United Kingdom
PII.description: UK-specific PII including NHS numbers for UK GDPR and NHS data governance compliance.

PII.name: Australia
PII.description: Australian identifiers: Tax File Numbers, Medicare cards, ABN, and ACN for Privacy Act 1988 compliance.

PII.name: Singapore
PII.description: Singapore identifiers: NRIC/FIN and UEN for PDPA compliance.

PII.name: India
PII.description: Indian identifiers: PAN, Aadhaar, voter IDs, and vehicle registration for DPDP Act 2023 compliance.

PII.name: Nordic (Finland & Poland)
PII.description: Finnish personal identity codes (HETU) and Polish PESEL numbers for local privacy law compliance.

PII.name: Healthcare / Clinical
PII.description: Health-sector PII: medical licenses, patient names, dates, clinic locations, and contact details. For clinical notes, patient records, and health system exports.

PII.name: Finance & Banking (PCI-DSS)
PII.description: Financial PII: credit cards, IBANs, crypto wallets, bank accounts, and tax identifiers. For PCI-DSS scope assessments and financial audits.

PII.name: High Confidence Only
PII.description: Maximum-precision scan across all entity types with elevated confidence threshold. For automated enforcement pipelines where false positives are costly.

PII.name: Custom — Employee ID Recognizer
PII.description: Extend PII detection with a regex recognizer for internal employee IDs (EMP-XXXXXX format). Shows how to detect proprietary identifiers alongside standard PII.

PII.name: Custom — Project Code Deny List
PII.description: Deny-list recognizer for internal code names that should be treated as sensitive in external communications.

YARA.name: Cloud Credentials
YARA.description: Hardcoded cloud credentials and API keys: AWS Access Keys, GitHub/GitLab tokens, Slack bot tokens, OpenAI keys, Stripe live keys, PEM private keys, and JWTs.

rules.name: AWS_Access_Key
rules.description: AWS Access Key ID (AKIA prefix)

rules.name: GitHub_Token
rules.description: GitHub Personal Access Token (ghp_, gho_, ghs_, ghu_, ghr_ prefix)

rules.name: GitLab_Token
rules.description: GitLab Personal Access or Deploy Token (glpat- prefix)

rules.name: Slack_Token
rules.description: Slack API token — bot (xoxb), app (xoxa), or user (xoxp)

rules.name: OpenAI_API_Key
rules.description: OpenAI API key (sk- prefix, 48+ chars)

rules.name: Stripe_Live_Key
rules.description: Stripe live secret key (sk_live_) or restricted key (rk_live_)

rules.name: PEM_Private_Key
rules.description: PEM-encoded private key block (RSA, EC, PKCS8, etc.)

rules.name: JWT_Token
rules.description: JSON Web Token — three base64url segments separated by dots

YARA.name: Malware Indicators
YARA.description: Windows process-injection APIs, known offensive tool names (mimikatz, msfvenom, CobaltStrike), and registry persistence patterns.

rules.name: Process_Injection_APIs
rules.description: Two or more Win32 process-injection APIs — indicates reflective DLL injection or process hollowing

rules.name: Known_Offensive_Tools
rules.description: References to well-known post-exploitation and credential-dump tools

rules.name: Registry_Persistence
rules.description: Windows registry run-key persistence or scheduled-task creation

YARA.name: Suspicious Scripts
YARA.description: PowerShell download-and-execute chains, base64-encoded commands, shell curl/wget pipe-to-exec, Python exec obfuscation, and JavaScript eval patterns.

rules.name: PowerShell_Download_Exec
rules.description: PowerShell download + execute cradle pattern

rules.name: PowerShell_Encoded_Command
rules.description: PowerShell base64 command encoding for evasion

rules.name: Shell_Curl_Pipe_Exec
rules.description: Shell: curl or wget piped into an interpreter

rules.name: Python_Exec_Obfuscation
rules.description: Python obfuscation combining exec/compile with encoding

rules.name: JavaScript_Eval_Obfuscation
rules.description: JavaScript obfuscation via eval and character encoding

YARA.name: Office Macro Threats
YARA.description: Malicious VBA macro patterns: auto-execution entry points, shell spawning via WScript.Shell, and network downloads via MSXML2.

rules.name: VBA_Auto_Execution
rules.description: VBA macro auto-execution entry points

rules.name: VBA_Shell_Execution
rules.description: VBA macro spawning cmd.exe or PowerShell

rules.name: VBA_Network_Download
rules.description: VBA macro downloading content via MSXML or WinHttp

YARA.name: PDF Threat Indicators
YARA.description: Suspicious PDF structure: embedded JavaScript, auto-open/launch actions, and form-submit/data-import streams.

rules.name: PDF_Embedded_JavaScript
rules.description: PDF with embedded JavaScript action

rules.name: PDF_Auto_Open_Action
rules.description: PDF auto-open, launch, or additional-action stream

rules.name: PDF_Form_Submit_Or_Import
rules.description: PDF form submission or external data import

YARA.name: Supply Chain Threats
YARA.description: Crypto miners, reverse shells, obfuscated npm/pip install hooks, and exfiltration infrastructure (ngrok, webhook.site, interactsh).

rules.name: Crypto_Miner_Indicators
rules.description: Crypto-miner tool names or mining pool protocol strings

rules.name: Reverse_Shell_Payload
rules.description: Reverse shell via /dev/tcp, nc -e, or socat+mkfifo

rules.name: Obfuscated_Install_Hook
rules.description: Obfuscated code in npm or pip install lifecycle hook

rules.name: Exfil_Infrastructure
rules.description: Known exfiltration services: ngrok, requestbin, webhook.site, burpcollaborator, interactsh

YARA.name: Network Threat Indicators
YARA.description: C2 framework artifacts (CobaltStrike, Metasploit), known RAT strings (QuasarRAT, AsyncRAT, NanoCore), and DNS-tunneling tools.

rules.name: CobaltStrike_Artifacts
rules.description: CobaltStrike beacon, ReflectiveDll, or Metasploit module path strings

rules.name: Known_RAT_Strings
rules.description: Well-known Remote Access Trojan artifact strings

rules.name: DNS_Tunneling_Tools
rules.description: DNS-tunneling tools used for data exfiltration over DNS

YARA.name: Credential Theft Patterns
YARA.description: Keylogger APIs, phishing credential-form HTML structure, and paste/file-transfer services used for credential exfiltration.

rules.name: Keylogger_APIs
rules.description: Windows keyboard-hook API references indicative of a keylogger

rules.name: Phishing_Credential_Form
rules.description: HTML form consistent with credential-harvesting phishing page

rules.name: Credential_Exfil_Services
rules.description: Anonymous paste/file-transfer services abused for credential exfiltration

YARA.name: Custom — Internal Token Format
YARA.description: Example custom YARA rule for detecting proprietary internal service tokens (SVC-XXXXXXXX). Combine with cloud credential rules for full coverage.

rules.name: Internal_Service_Token
rules.description: Internal service token in SVC-XXXXXXXX format

rules.name: GitHub_Token
rules.description: GitHub Personal Access Token

BROKEN_LINKS.name: Default Broken Links
BROKEN_LINKS.description: Check all links in the asset for reachability with default settings.

BROKEN_LINKS.name: Capped Scan
BROKEN_LINKS.description: Limit findings to the first 100 broken links to keep reports concise.

CODE_SECURITY.name: Code Security Baseline
CODE_SECURITY.description: Scan code snippets for insecure patterns using Bandit — catches common Python security issues like SQL injection, hardcoded passwords, and unsafe deserialization.

CUSTOM.name: Support Ticket Extractor
CUSTOM.description: Extract order IDs, monetary amounts, and customer intent from support tickets using GLINER2 NER + classification.

config.name: Support Ticket Extractor
config.description: Extract order IDs, amounts, and intent from support tickets

CUSTOM.name: Regulatory Entity Extractor (DACH)
CUSTOM.description: Extract regulatory bodies, GDPR article references, and legal risk terms from German/English documents using GLINER2.

config.name: DACH Regulatory Entity Extractor
config.description: Extract regulatory entities (GDPR articles, supervisory bodies, risk terms)

regulatory_body.description: Supervisory authority like BaFin, FINMA, FMA

CUSTOM.name: Contract Risk Classifier (DACH)
CUSTOM.description: Classify contract clauses by legal risk category — liability, data protection, termination, indemnification. Multi-label classification with GLINER2.

config.name: DACH Contract Risk Terms
config.description: Detect legal/compliance risk language in DACH contracts

CUSTOM.name: Internal Product Code Detector
CUSTOM.description: Detect internal SKU codes (INT-OPS-12345) and project codes (PROJ-AB12CD34) leaking into public documents using regex patterns.

config.name: Internal Product Code Detector
config.description: Detect internal SKU and project code leakage in public docs

CUSTOM.name: German Invoice PII (Regex)
CUSTOM.description: Detect German IBANs, postal codes with city names, and DD.MM.YYYY dates in invoices and financial documents.

CUSTOM.name: API Key Leakage Detector (Regex)
CUSTOM.description: Detect generic API key assignments, Bearer tokens, and base64-encoded secrets in documents and code snippets.

config.name: API Key Leakage Detector
config.description: Detect leaked API keys, tokens, and secrets in documents and code snippets

CUSTOM.name: Sarcasm & Irony Detector
CUSTOM.description: Classify text as sarcastic, ironic, or sincere — useful for filtering user-generated content before sentiment analysis.

config.name: Sarcasm & Irony Detector
config.description: Detect sarcasm and irony in user-generated content

CUSTOM.name: Vendor & Supplier Extractor
CUSTOM.description: Extract vendor names, contract values, and Incoterms delivery terms from procurement documents.

config.name: Vendor & Supplier Entity Extractor
config.description: Extract vendor names, contract values, and delivery terms

CUSTOM.name: Spam Detector
CUSTOM.description: Classify text as spam or legitimate using a lightweight BERT model — ideal for filtering user-generated content and form submissions.

CUSTOM.name: Sentiment Analyzer
CUSTOM.description: Classify text as positive or negative sentiment using DistilBERT — useful for customer feedback analysis.

CUSTOM.name: NSFW Image Detector
CUSTOM.description: Detect sexually explicit or inappropriate images for content moderation in document pipelines.

CUSTOM.name: NSFW Image Detector (ViT-Tiny)
CUSTOM.description: Compact ViT-tiny NSFW classifier via Marqo/nsfw-image-detection-384 — smaller and faster than the base NSFW detector, well-suited for CPU-only or high-throughput document image screening.

config.name: NSFW Image Detector (ViT-Tiny)
config.description: Lightweight ViT-tiny NSFW classifier for content moderation

CUSTOM.name: General Image Classifier (ViT)
CUSTOM.description: Baseline ImageNet-1k classification using ViT — returns top-5 predicted labels for document image categorization.

CUSTOM.name: Semantic Embeddings (BGE)
CUSTOM.description: Produce normalized sentence embeddings with BAAI/bge-base for semantic search and RAG pipelines.

CUSTOM.name: Lightweight Embeddings (MiniLM)
CUSTOM.description: Fast, small embeddings via MiniLM — ideal for CPU inference and high-throughput pipelines.

CUSTOM.name: Multilingual Embeddings (E5)
CUSTOM.description: Multilingual embeddings using E5 — for enterprise content in multiple languages.

CUSTOM.name: General Object Detection (DETR)
CUSTOM.description: Detect common objects in images using DETR — useful for automated document image categorization.

CUSTOM.name: Security Screening (People & Weapons)
CUSTOM.description: Flag images containing persons, weapons, or vehicles for content moderation and physical security screening.

CUSTOM.name: Realtime Object Detection (RT-DETR)
CUSTOM.description: Production-grade realtime detection with RT-DETR — for high-throughput image processing pipelines.

CUSTOM.name: Financial Sentiment (FinBERT)
CUSTOM.description: Classify financial text — earnings calls, news articles, analyst reports — as positive, negative, or neutral using ProsusAI/finbert. Trained on financial phrase bank; negative findings surface for review in risk workflows.

config.name: Financial Sentiment (FinBERT)
config.description: Detect negative or uncertain sentiment in financial documents

CUSTOM.name: Social Media Sentiment (RoBERTa)
CUSTOM.description: Sentiment analysis tuned for social media language — abbreviations, hashtags, informal phrasing — using cardiffnlp/twitter-roberta-base-sentiment-latest. Negative signals elevated to medium for brand monitoring pipelines.

config.name: Social Media Sentiment (RoBERTa)
config.description: Detect negative or neutral sentiment in social media and user-generated content

CUSTOM.name: Language Detector (XLM-RoBERTa)
CUSTOM.description: Identify the language of any text asset across ~20 languages using papluca/xlm-roberta-base-language-detection. Useful for routing assets to the correct PII recognizer or flagging unexpected languages in compliance-controlled data stores.

config.name: Language Detector (XLM-RoBERTa)
config.description: Detect the language of document content for routing and compliance

CUSTOM.name: Toxic Content Detector
CUSTOM.description: Flag toxic, hateful, or abusive language in user-generated content using a binary RoBERTa-base toxicity classifier. Suited for moderation queues, forum exports, and internal communication audits where unsafe content must be quarantined before review.

config.name: Toxic Content Detector
config.description: Detect toxic and abusive language in user-generated content using a binary RoBERTa-base toxicity classifier

CUSTOM.name: High-Quality Embeddings (MPNet)
CUSTOM.description: Dense sentence embeddings via sentence-transformers/all-mpnet-base-v2 — 768-dimensional vectors optimized for semantic similarity and document retrieval. Stronger than MiniLM at the cost of higher latency; recommended for production RAG indexes.

config.name: High-Quality Embeddings (MPNet)
config.description: 768-dimensional sentence embeddings for semantic search and RAG

CUSTOM.name: Retrieval Embeddings (GTE Large)
CUSTOM.description: High-ranking general-text embeddings via thenlper/gte-large — consistently top-performing on the MTEB retrieval benchmark. Use for semantic search over large document corpora where recall quality matters more than latency.

config.name: Retrieval Embeddings (GTE Large)
config.description: MTEB top-performing embeddings for retrieval and re-ranking

CUSTOM.name: CNN Image Classifier (ResNet-50)
CUSTOM.description: Classic ImageNet-1k convolutional baseline via microsoft/resnet-50. Faster than ViT on CPU and well-understood; useful as a lightweight sanity check or when a simple 1,000-class label is sufficient for document image triage.

config.name: CNN Image Classifier (ResNet-50)
config.description: Lightweight ImageNet-1k classification via ResNet-50 CNN

CUSTOM.name: Efficient Vision Classifier (DeiT)
CUSTOM.description: Data-efficient image transformer via facebook/deit-base-distilled-patch16-224 — matches ViT accuracy with a distillation trick that requires less training data. Good balance of accuracy and throughput for ImageNet-1k classification on CPU.

config.name: Efficient Vision Classifier (DeiT)
config.description: Data-efficient image transformer for ImageNet-1k classification

CUSTOM.name: Broad-Category Classifier (BEiT 22k)
CUSTOM.description: ImageNet-22k classification via microsoft/beit-base-patch16-224-pt22k-ft22k — 21,841 fine-grained categories covering animals, vehicles, plants, food, and objects. Use when the 1,000-class label set is too coarse for your categorization workflow.

config.name: Broad-Category Classifier (BEiT 22k)
config.description: Fine-grained 21k-class image classification for detailed document image triage

CUSTOM.name: Lightweight Object Detection (YOLOS)
CUSTOM.description: Compact ViT-based object detector via hustvl/yolos-small — pre-trained on ImageNet, fine-tuned on COCO 2017. Lower memory footprint than DETR; suitable for edge inference or high-throughput pipelines where DETR is too heavy.

config.name: Lightweight Object Detection (YOLOS)
config.description: Small vision-transformer object detector for COCO 80-class detection

CUSTOM.name: Tiny Object Detection (YOLOS)
CUSTOM.description: Ultra-lightweight ViT-based object detector via hustvl/yolos-tiny — ~6M parameters, pre-trained on ImageNet and fine-tuned on COCO 2017. Ideal for CPU-only or desktop deployments where a smaller footprint matters more than top accuracy.

config.name: Tiny Object Detection (YOLOS)
config.description: Ultra-lightweight vision-transformer object detector for CPU/desktop use

CUSTOM.name: Conditional DETR Detector
CUSTOM.description: End-to-end COCO object detection via microsoft/conditional-detr-resnet-50 — faster convergence than original DETR thanks to conditional cross-attention. Use as an alternative to DETR when you need faster training for fine-tuning on domain-specific classes.

config.name: Conditional DETR Detector
config.description: Conditional cross-attention DETR for COCO 80-class object detection
